title: site_review_verdict
description: Verdict produced by the site-review story's agent.decide call. Pinned into world.verdict and written to .artifacts/site-review-<mode>-<sha>.json. The shape renders as a listing: one row per gap between what shipped and what the feature catalog / product site advertises, with 1+ evidence records explaining the commit/source that opened the gap.

decision.description: Binary verdict — needs_update means at least one shipped capability or site surface is out of sync.

summary.description: One-paragraph rationale; cites feature ids, file paths, and commit short-shas.

commits.description: Every commit considered in this audit. Used as the lookup table referenced by gaps[].evidence[].commit. May be empty only when the verdict fell back to a structural audit.

capability.description: One sentence on the user-visible capability this commit delivered. Not a restatement of the subject.

gaps.description: One record per gap between what shipped and what the catalog/site shows. Empty array when decision == up_to_date.

kind.description: missing-catalog-entry: a shipped capability has no features/<id>.yaml. missing-demo: a cataloged feature has no recordable demo binding. site-inconsistency: catalog/site metadata disagrees with reality (e.g. a docs link outside the allowlist, a promoted feature missing required pieces). stale-doc: published site copy under tools/site/src/** (or the docs/** it is generated from) contradicts the code.

target.description: The feature id (for catalog/demo gaps) or repo-relative file path (for site/doc gaps) the gap is about.

lines.description: Optional line range inside the cited file, e.g. '110-117', when the gap is localized.

detail.description: Optional one-line elaboration shown in the listing.

evidence.description: 1+ records of what shipped (a commit, or a source file) such that the target is now missing or wrong. One change per record.

commit.description: SHA from commits[], or the literal 'working-tree' for uncommitted changes, or 'structural' when no specific commit applies.

source.description: Optional file/path that drives the gap (e.g. 'stories/deck-review/app.yaml' or 'tools/site/docs-manifest.json'). Cite when it sharpens the link.

change.description: What the commit / source delivered or changed at the behavior or contract level.

reason.description: Why this makes the target a gap. Quote or paraphrase the catalog/site's current state.

recommended_actions.description: Short directive strings, ideally one per gaps[] entry in the same order.
